contextual.title: Physical Activity for Advanced Stage Cancer Patients
contextual.text:
Summary: The purpose of this study is to understand if a home-based physical activity program can improve symptoms and physical function among patients with advanced stage cancer.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 have stage III or IV cancer diagnosis 

 able to understand English 

 have a Karnofsky Performance Status (KPS) score of 60% (able to care for most of the personal needs) 

 have fatigue and/or two other target symptoms 

 have been cleared by their provider to engage low to moderate intensity physical activities 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 are hospitalized 

 have lesion or metastasis of bone 

 have a cardiac pacemaker 

 have a history of seizure or loss of consciousness 

 have been using a Wii Fit 

 diagnosed cognitive impairment

Cancer.definition: A neoplasm composed of atypical neoplastic, often pleomorphic cells that invade other tissues. Malignant neoplasms often metastasize to distant anatomic sites and may recur after excision. The most common malignant neoplasms are carcinomas, Hodgkin and non-Hodgkin lymphomas, leukemias, melanomas, and sarcomas.
